section_title: Statutory Appendix: Verbatim Text of Key PMLA and PML Rules Provisions

content_blocks.text:
## 18. Statutory Appendix: Verbatim Text of Key PMLA and PML Rules Provisions

### 18.1 Purpose and Scope of This Appendix

This Appendix reproduces the verbatim text of the principal statutory provisions cited throughout this Policy. The text is reproduced from authoritative primary sources, specifically the official notifications page of the Financial Intelligence Unit – India (https://fiuindia.gov.in/files/AML_Legislation/notification.html) and the consolidated reproduction of the gazette publications available on Indian Kanoon (https://indiankanoon.org).

The purpose of this Appendix is to enable the reader of this Policy to verify, at the point of citation, the precise statutory text underlying each operational requirement. The Appendix is intended to support regulatory defensibility, internal audit, training, and qualified legal review of this Policy. Inclusion of statutory text in this Appendix does not constitute interpretation of the law and is not legal advice.

Where a statutory provision has been amended after the date of this Policy, the current text of the provision shall prevail over the text reproduced here. The user of this Policy is responsible for verifying the current text of each cited provision before reliance.

### 18.2 Sources

(a) **Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002 (PMLA)** — as published by the Ministry of Finance, Government of India, and amended from time to time. Verbatim text available at indiacode.nic.in and reproduced on Indian Kanoon.

(b) **Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005 (PML Rules)** — as published by the Ministry of Finance, Government of India, and amended from time to time. Verbatim text available at https://fiuindia.gov.in/files/AML_Legislation/notification.html (official FIU-IND source) and reproduced on Indian Kanoon.

### 18.3 PMLA Section 2(1)(wa) — Reporting Entity

**Source:** Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002, Section 2(1)(wa) as inserted with effect from 15 February 2013.

> *"'reporting entity' means a banking company, financial institution, intermediary or a person carrying on a designated business or profession;"*

**Relevance to this Policy:** The Company qualifies as a Reporting Entity under this definition by virtue of carrying on a designated business or profession (VDA service provision) as notified by the Ministry of Finance vide Notification S.O. 1072(E) dated March 7, 2023. See Sections 2 and 4 of this Policy.

### 18.4 PMLA Section 3 — Offence of Money Laundering

**Source:** Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002, Section 3 (as amended).

> *"Whosoever directly or indirectly attempts to indulge or knowingly assists or knowingly is a party or is actually involved in any process or activity connected with the proceeds of crime including its concealment, possession, acquisition or use and projecting or claiming it as untainted property shall be guilty of offence of money-laundering."*

**Relevance to this Policy:** This is the foundational offence under PMLA. The Company's AML/CFT/CPF framework set out in this Policy is designed to prevent, detect, and report activities that may constitute, facilitate, or arise from money laundering as defined here. See Section 3 of this Policy (Definitions) and all operational sections.

### 18.5 PMLA Section 12 — Reporting Entity to Maintain Records

**Source:** Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002, Section 12 (as amended).

> *"(1) Every reporting entity shall —*
> *(a) maintain a record of all transactions, including information relating to transactions covered under clause (b), in such manner as to enable it to reconstruct individual transactions;*
> *(b) furnish to the Director within such time as may be prescribed, information relating to such transactions, whether attempted or executed, the nature and value of which may be prescribed;*
> *(c) ... [verify the identity of its clients in such manner and subject to such conditions, as may be prescribed];*
> *(d) ... [identify the beneficial owner, if any, of such of its clients, as may be prescribed];*
> *(e) maintain record of documents evidencing identity of its clients and beneficial owners as well as account files and business correspondence relating to its clients.*
>
> *(2) Every information maintained, furnished or verified, save as otherwise provided under any law for the time being in force, shall be kept confidential.*
>
> *(3) The records referred to in clause (a) of sub-section (1) shall be maintained for a period of five years from the date of transaction between a client and the reporting entity.*
>
> *(4) The records referred to in clause (e) of sub-section (1) shall be maintained for a period of five years after the business relationship between a client and the reporting entity has ended or the account has been closed, whichever is later."*

**Relevance to this Policy:** Section 12 is the foundational record-keeping obligation under PMLA. Section 12(2) is the foundational confidentiality obligation that underpins the tipping-off prohibition. See Sections 15.2 and 16 of this Policy.

### 18.6 PMLA Section 12A — Access to Information

**Source:** Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002, Section 12A (as inserted).

> *"(1) The Director may call for from any reporting entity any of the records referred to in sub-section (1) of section 12 and any additional information as he considers necessary for the purposes of this Act.*
>
> *(2) Every reporting entity shall furnish to the Director such information as may be required by him under sub-section (1) within such time and in such manner as he may specify.*
>
> *(3) Save as otherwise provided under any law for the time being in force, every information sought by the Director under sub-section (1), shall be kept confidential."*

**Relevance to this Policy:** Section 12A is the basis for the Company's obligation to respond to information requests from FIU-IND. The confidentiality obligation under Section 12A(3) reinforces the tipping-off prohibition. See Sections 15.2 and 15.3.3 of this Policy.

### 18.7 PMLA Section 13 — Powers of Director to Impose Fine

**Source:** Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002, Section 13 (as amended).

> *"(1) The Director may, either of his own motion or on an application made by any authority, officer or person, call for records referred to in sub-section (1) of section 12 and may make such inquiry or cause such inquiry to be made, as he thinks fit.*
>
> *(1A) If at any stage of inquiry or any other proceedings before him, the Director having regard to the nature and complexity of the case, is of the opinion that it is necessary to do so, he may direct the concerned reporting entity to get its records, as may be specified, audited by an accountant from amongst a panel of accountants, maintained by the Central Government for this purpose.*
>
> *(2) If the Director, in the course of any inquiry, finds that a reporting entity or its designated director on the Board or any of its employees has failed to comply with the obligations under this Chapter, then, without prejudice to any other action that may be taken under any other provisions of this Act, he may —*
> *(a) issue a warning in writing; or*
> *(b) direct such reporting entity or its designated director on the Board or any of its employees, to comply with specific instructions; or*
> *(c) direct such reporting entity or its designated director on the Board or any of its employees, to send reports at such interval as may be prescribed on the measures it is taking; or*
> *(d) by an order, impose a monetary penalty on such reporting entity or its designated director on the Board or any of its employees, which shall not be less than ten thousand rupees but may extend to one lakh rupees for each failure."*

**Relevance to this Policy:** Section 13 is the basis for FIU-IND's enforcement powers, including the imposition of monetary penalties for non-compliance. The Company's adherence to the framework set out in this Policy is intended to ensure compliance with the obligations whose breach would attract Section 13 action. See Section 4 of this Policy (Registration) and all operational sections.

### 18.8 PML Rules Rule 2(1)(ba) — Designated Director

**Source:** Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, Rule 2(1)(ba) as inserted by the Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Amendment Rules, 2013.

> *"'Designated Director' means a person designated by the reporting entity to ensure overall compliance with the obligations imposed under chapter IV of the Act and the Rules and includes —*
>
> *(i) the Managing Director or a whole-time Director duly authorized by the Board of Directors if the reporting entity is a company,*
>
> *(ii) the managing partner if the reporting entity is a partnership firm,*
>
> *(iii) the proprietor if the reporting entity is a proprietorship concern,*
>
> *(iv) the managing trustee if the reporting entity is a trust,*
>
> *(v) a person or individual, as the case may be, who controls and manages the affairs of the reporting entity if the reporting entity is an unincorporated association or a body of individuals, and*
>
> *(vi) such other person or class of persons as may be notified by the Government if the reporting entity does not fall in any of the categories above.*
>
> *Explanation. — For the purpose of this clause, the terms 'Managing Director' and 'Whole-time Director' shall have the meaning assigned to them in the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013)."*

**Relevance to this Policy:** Rule 2(1)(ba) is the statutory definition of Designated Director referenced in Section 5.1 of this Policy. The Company's Designated Director shall fall within one of the sub-clauses (i) to (vi) above.

### 18.9 PML Rules Rule 2(1)(f) — Principal Officer

**Source:** Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, Rule 2(1)(f).

> *"'Principal Officer' means an officer designated by a reporting entity;*
>
> *Provided that such officer shall be an officer at the management level."*

**Relevance to this Policy:** Rule 2(1)(f) is the statutory definition of Principal Officer referenced in Section 5.2 of this Policy. The minimum requirements and qualifications applicable to the Principal Officer are set out in Paragraph 3.2 of the 2026 FIU-IND Guidelines and reproduced operationally in Section 5.2.2 of this Policy.

### 18.10 PML Rules Rule 7 — Procedure and Manner of Furnishing Information

**Source:** Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, Rule 7 (as amended).

> *"(1) Every reporting entity shall communicate to the Director the name, designation and address of the Designated Director and the Principal Officer.*
>
> *(2) The Principal Officer shall furnish the information referred to in clauses (A), (B), (BA), (C), (D), (E) and (F) of sub-rule (1) of rule 3 to the Director on the basis of information available with the reporting entity.*
>
> *(3) Every reporting entity may evolve an internal mechanism having regard to any guidelines issued by regulator, for detecting the transactions referred to in clauses (A), (B), (BA), (C), (D), (E) and (F) of sub-rule (1) of rule 3 and for furnishing information about such transactions in such form and at such intervals as may be directed by its regulator.*
>
> *(4) It shall be the duty of every reporting entity, its designated director, officers and employees to observe the procedure and the manner of furnishing information as specified by the Director in consultation with its Regulator."*

**Relevance to this Policy:** Rule 7 is the foundational statutory basis for:

- The Company's obligation to notify the names of the Designated Director and Principal Officer to FIU-IND via the FINGate portal (Sections 4.4, 5.1.1, 5.2.1 of this Policy);

- The Principal Officer's obligation to furnish prescribed transaction information to FIU-IND (Section 15 of this Policy);

- The Company's obligation to evolve an internal detection and reporting mechanism (Section 6 of this Policy).

### 18.11 PML Rules Rule 8 — Furnishing of Information to the Director

**Source:** Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, Rule 8 (as amended).

> *"(1) The Principal Officer of a reporting entity shall furnish the information in respect of transactions referred to in clauses (A), (B), (BA), (C) and (E) of sub-rule (1) of rule 3 every month to the Director by the 15th day of the succeeding month.*
>
> *(2) The Principal Officer of a reporting entity shall furnish the information promptly in writing or by fax or by electronic mail to the Director in respect of transactions referred to in clause (D) of sub-rule (1) of rule 3 not later than seven working days on being satisfied that the transaction is suspicious.*
>
> *(3) The Principal Officer of a reporting entity shall furnish the information in respect of transactions referred to in clause (F) of sub-rule (1) of rule 3, every quarter to the Director by the 15th day of the month following the quarter.*
>
> *(6) The Principal Officer of the reporting entity shall ensure to keep the maintenance of records and furnishing of information referred to in clauses (A), (B), (BA), (C), (D), (E) and (F) of sub-rule (1) of rule 3 strictly confidential and the said information shall not be communicated to the client or any other person."*

**Relevance to this Policy:**

- Rule 8(1) is the basis for monthly reporting to FIU-IND (Section 15.3.1 of this Policy);

- Rule 8(2) is the basis for the obligation to file STRs within seven working days of being satisfied that a transaction is suspicious (Section 15.1 of this Policy);

- Rule 8(6) is the foundational statutory basis for the tipping-off prohibition (Section 15.2 of this Policy).

### 18.12 PML Rules Rule 9 — Verification of Identity of Clients

**Source:** Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, Rule 9 (as amended). Rule 9 is the foundational CDD provision and contains multiple sub-rules. The most operationally significant sub-rules cited in this Policy are reproduced below.

> *"(1) Every reporting entity shall —*
>
> *(a) at the time of commencement of an account-based relationship, identify its clients, verify their identity and obtain information on the purpose and intended nature of the business relationship, and*
>
> *(b) in all other cases, verify identity while carrying out —*
> *(i) transaction of an amount equal to or exceeding rupees fifty thousand, whether conducted as a single transaction or several transactions that appear to be connected, or*
> *(ii) any international money transfer operations."*

> *"(3) Every reporting entity shall identify the beneficial owner and take all reasonable steps to verify his identity."*

> *"(12) Every reporting entity shall exercise ongoing due diligence with respect to the business relationship with every client and closely examine the transactions in order to ensure that they are consistent with their knowledge of the customer, his business and risk profile and where necessary, the source of funds."*

> *"(13) Every reporting entity shall carry out risk assessment to identify, assess and take effective measures to mitigate its money laundering and terrorist financing risk for clients, countries or geographic areas, and products, services, transactions or delivery channels that is consistent with any national risk assessment conducted by a body or authority duly notified by the Central Government."*

> *"(14) Reporting entity shall apply a Risk Based Approach for mitigation and management of the identified risk and should have Board approved policies, controls and procedures, duly documented, to enable them to manage and mitigate the risk that have been identified either by the reporting entity or through national risk assessment.*
>
> *Such policies, controls and procedures shall be reviewed periodically to ensure their effectiveness. Further, in order to ensure the effectiveness of policies and procedures, the person doing such a review shall be different from the one who has framed such policies and procedures."*

**Relevance to this Policy:**

- Rule 9(1) is the foundational CDD obligation (Sections 10 and 11 of this Policy);

- Rule 9(3) is the basis for beneficial ownership identification (Section 11.1.2 of this Policy);

- Rule 9(12) is the basis for Ongoing Due Diligence (Section 12.1 of this Policy);

- Rule 9(13) is the basis for periodic Risk Assessment (Section 8.1 of this Policy);

- Rule 9(14) is the basis for the Risk-Based Approach and the requirement for independent review of policies and procedures (Sections 6.3 and 8.1 of this Policy).

### 18.13 Closing Note on This Appendix

The ten statutory provisions reproduced above represent the principal statutory anchoring of the operational requirements set out in this Policy. Additional statutory provisions cited in this Policy (including but not limited to Sections 2(1)(sa)(vi), Chapter IV of the PMLA; Rules 2(1)(cba), 2(1)(cf), 2(1)(db), 3, 3A, 4, 5, 9(9A), 10(3) of the PML Rules) are referenced at the point of citation in the relevant section of this Policy but are not reproduced verbatim in this Appendix. The user of this Policy is referred to the official text of these provisions available on the FIU-IND notifications page (https://fiuindia.gov.in/files/AML_Legislation/notification.html) and Indian Kanoon (https://indiankanoon.org).

The statutory framework is supplemented by:

(a) Ministry of Finance designating notifications (S.O. 1072(E) dated March 7, 2023; Notification dated July 4, 2023; S.O. 4877(E) dated November 9, 2023);

(b) FIU-IND AML & CFT Guidelines for Reporting Entities Providing Services Related to Virtual Digital Assets dated January 8, 2026, which constitute the principal operational guidance under which this Policy is framed;

(c) Other applicable laws and directives referenced in Section 2 of this Policy.

This Appendix is informational and is intended to facilitate verification of statutory text by readers of this Policy. It is not a substitute for direct consultation of the official statutory text or qualified legal advice.
